bibliographic_data.title: Pokémon GO to Pokémon STAY: How Covid-19 Affected Pokémon GO Players
bibliographic_data.abstract: Since its creation, the Location-Based Game (LBG), Pokémon GO, has been embraced by a community of fans across the world. Due to its recency, the impact of COVID-19 on the community of Pokémon GO players is underexplored. We address how COVID-19 has impacted the players of Pokémon GO by building upon existing work focusing on player gratifications and impacts in Pokémon GO. Through semi-structured interviews, we provide a snapshot of the state of LBG play during unprecedented times. These player testimonies demonstrate (1) the importance of in-person socialization to LBG, (2) additional ways players use the game as a coping mechanism, and (3) how intentionality mediates player perceptions of people-place relationships. In demonstrating these behaviors, we provide a glimpse of how a game that forces players to explore the world around them changed when going outside with friends became a source of danger.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study explores the impact of COVID-19 on Pokémon GO players, focusing on socialization changes, coping mechanisms, and redefined people-place relationships.
core_content.key_methodology: Semi-structured interviews conducted via Zoom with thirty participants.
outcomes: Perceived changes in socialization from in-person to digital interaction
Use of the game as a coping mechanism during the pandemic
Redefined perceptions of space and place due to the pandemic
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study investigates how the Location-Based Game (LBG) Pokémon GO was affected by the COVID-19 pandemic. Through semi-structured interviews, it explores changes in socialization from in-person to digital interaction, the use of the game as a coping mechanism, and redefined perceptions of space and place. The research contributes insights into the importance of mobility, space, and socialization in LBGs.
